items.title: Enhance Kling API Documentation in Skills 🎉
items.summary: This update improves the documentation for the Kling lip-sync API, specifically the `/kling/lip-sync` endpoint. Key changes include a detailed description of lip-sync capabilities, request parameters, and an illustrative example for users. The modifications ensure that the skill guidance is aligned with the latest OpenAPI specifications.

items.title: Fix voiceover endpoint and references in Skills 🎥
items.summary: This update corrects the voiceover endpoint for the marketing-video-factory skill, changing it to the accurate OpenAI-compatible route `/v1/audio/speech`. Additionally, references to non-existent files have been updated to point to the correct scripts and materials. These changes enhance the functionality and reliability of the promo video generation process.

items.title: New Offline Digital Human Deployment Plans in Index 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive research and deployment plan for an offline digital human solution, allowing users to create videos by uploading photos, voice samples, and scripts. Key documents include a detailed research solution guide, Windows deployment instructions for RTX 3090, and an automated deployment script. The project aims to establish a self-hosted version of HeyGen, optimizing for local processing without reliance on online APIs.

items.title: Enhance Dreamina Worker with AK/SK Environment Variables in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `VOLCENGINE_ACCESS_KEY` and `VOLCENGINE_SECRET_KEY` environment variables to the seedance and seedream worker deployments. These keys are sourced from the `volcengine` secret and are essential for signing the Visual `cv` API, ensuring a secure connection distinct from the Ark Bearer `API_KEY`. This change has already been applied live and will persist across future deployments.

items.title: Revamped Video Factory Plan for Fish TTS in Index 🎥
items.summary: This update corrects the architecture for the article-to-video factory to utilize the existing Fish TTS endpoint for voiceovers. Key changes include the removal of outdated assumptions regarding TTSMCP and the establishment of scene-level reliability measures. The focus is now on enhancing health monitoring and retry behavior rather than creating a duplicate API, streamlining the implementation process.

items.title: Automated Video Marketing Material Factory in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces an evidence-driven automated video marketing design that enhances material collection, creative compilation, and distribution processes. Key changes include the integration of a new TTS provider, Fish, and the addition of a comprehensive material catalog sourced from various documentation and real API outputs. The deployment guidance has also been updated to ensure a health check for the TTS service before publishing.

items.title: Introducing the Talking Photo Tab for Kling in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update adds a new Talking Photo tab to the Kling app, allowing users to create talking videos from a portrait and audio with a single click. The feature is powered by a new API endpoint and includes enhancements to the frontend, such as a dedicated panel for uploads and internationalization support for both English and Chinese. Overall, this addition significantly enhances user experience by simplifying the video creation process.

items.title: Boost default max_tokens for reasoning models in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the default `max_tokens` from 200 to 2048 in the API Playground, addressing issues with reasoning models like `gemini-3.0-pro` that require more tokens for internal processing. The change ensures that users can see complete responses without empty outputs, while still providing a clear teaching example. Non-reasoning models remain unaffected by this adjustment.

items.title: boost output budget for thinking models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the output budget for the E2E probe from 64 to 1024 tokens for thinking models like `gemini-3.0-pro`. The previous limit caused probes to report empty results due to internal reasoning consuming all tokens. By increasing the budget, we ensure that visible answers can be emitted while maintaining harmless checks for non-reasoning models.

items.title: Exciting New Talking Photo Feature in Backend 🎉
items.summary: We've introduced a public POST endpoint for the Kling Talking Photo API, allowing users to create talking videos from an image and audio. This feature integrates image2video and lip-sync technology, with bundle pricing based on duration. Additionally, cost tests have been implemented to ensure accuracy in pricing.

items.title: Introducing One-Shot Talking Photo Feature in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: We've added a new one-shot talking photo feature that allows users to generate a talking video from a single image and audio URL using the `/kling/talking-photo` endpoint. This feature simplifies the process by chaining image-to-video and lip-sync functionalities into one call, enhancing the Nexior 'talking photo' experience. It supports various parameters for customization and can be used as a standalone API.

items.title: Enhance Video Production with Material Library & Playbook in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive material library and a video production playbook to streamline our video creation process. The material library includes real product screenshots, generated brand stills, and finished sample videos, all indexed for easy access. The playbook outlines best practices and provides a reusable scene template for creating impactful one-minute feature promos.

items.title: Enhance TTS Billing Accuracy and Deploy Updates in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the billing issue for the TTS service by ensuring that the `x-billing-byte_count` header reflects the input size, allowing costs to scale appropriately. Additionally, it re-establishes the deployment and service configurations for the audio-speech functionality, ensuring they are reproducible for future deployments. The endpoint is now live and verified to bill correctly based on input size.

items.title: Launch of Marketing Video Factory Skill in Skills 🎥
items.summary: We've introduced a new workflow skill that transforms product features into engaging short-form videos. This playbook details the entire process, from strategy and asset generation to video assembly and distribution, leveraging existing media skills. Key updates include modifications to the marketplace registration and documentation to support this new skill.

items.title: Launch of Marketing Video Factory Design in Index 🎥
items.summary: We've introduced the design document for automating AceData's marketing videos, detailing strategies, tooling comparisons, and a storyboard for our first video. Additionally, the PlatformStudio submodule has been updated to include essential resources and earlier commits. The first short video has been successfully produced using our APIs, showcasing our capabilities.

items.title: Launch of Marketing Video Material Library in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive marketing-video material library, complete with a brand kit, verbatim copy bank, and AI-generated B-roll. Key tools for capturing UI screenshots and rendering videos have been added, enabling the production of our first short entirely using our APIs. The new resources will streamline future video creation and enhance our marketing efforts.

items.title: Enhance Kling Lip-Sync Descriptions in Backend 🎤
items.summary: This update converts 15 hardcoded description fields in the Kling lip-sync API to translatable references using the `$t()` function. The changes improve localization support by adding a Chinese translation block. Overall, this enhances the API's usability for a broader audience.

items.title: Enhance Long-Form Channel Edits in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a light-editing approach for long-form channels, ensuring that the original body, structure, and all images are preserved while only the title is rewritten. The `max_tokens` limit has been adjusted to prevent truncation of content, and the image-regression retry mechanism has been improved to maintain all source images. These changes will enhance the quality and consistency of generated materials.

items.title: Enhance CLI with --async Flag for Direct Commands in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `--async` flag to eight direct CLIs, allowing users to submit tasks asynchronously and receive a task ID for polling results. The change impacts commands like hailuo, kling, and luma, enhancing user experience by eliminating the need for a webhook callback. Additionally, the `--async` option is now included in the help documentation for better visibility.

items.title: Enhance Kling Lip-Sync with Voice Speed Control in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `voice_speed` parameter for the /kling/lip-sync text2video mode, allowing users to adjust the speed between 0.8 and 2.0. This addition completes the parameter set for comprehensive documentation, enhancing the flexibility and usability of the lip-sync feature.

items.title: Enhance Dify Plugins with Async Support in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `async` parameter to all 16 async-capable Dify plugins, aligning with the new API-level `async: true` flag. Key modifications include updates to YAML configurations, tool scripts, and client handling to ensure consistent async behavior across the board. This enhancement is part of a broader rollout aimed at improving asynchronous processing capabilities within the Dify ecosystem.

items.title: Revamped Kling Lip Sync Guide and OpenAPI Enhancements in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update features a comprehensive rewrite of the Kling Lip Sync integration guide, now including detailed parameters, examples, and a complete talking-photo pipeline. Additionally, the OpenAPI specification has been enhanced with new parameters such as audio_file, voice_speed, and voice_language enums, improving the overall functionality and usability of the API.

items.title: Fix pricing and routing issues for Kling Motion Control in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves two critical issues with the Kling Motion Control service. The routing error has been fixed, allowing access to the endpoint, and the pricing model has been updated to reflect per-second billing, aligning with official rates. These changes ensure accurate billing and improved service accessibility for customers.

items.title: Launch OpenAI-Compatible TTS Service in Backend 🎤
items.summary: We've successfully registered an OpenAI-compatible Text-to-Speech service, enabling the `POST /v1/audio/speech` endpoint for seamless audio synthesis. Key updates include new billing structures, comprehensive documentation in both English and Chinese, and a robust testing suite with 787 tests passed. This service offers competitive pricing, approximately 15% lower than OpenAI's rates, enhancing accessibility for users.

items.title: Enhance Dreamina with Translatable Descriptions in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update converts 15 hardcoded description fields in the Dreamina videos API to translatable references using the `$t()` function. It includes a new translations block for zh-cn, aligning with the all-services rollout strategy. These changes improve localization support and enhance the user experience for non-English speakers.

items.title: Introducing OpenAI-Compatible TTS Endpoint in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: We've added a new OpenAI-compatible text-to-speech endpoint at `/v1/audio/speech`, powered by the ephone worker. This feature allows for synchronous audio streaming with billing based on request size, enhancing our platform's capabilities. Key changes include the addition of the `OpenAISpeechHandler` and updates to the server routing.

items.title: Enhance Kling Lip Sync with Cost Tests and Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces cost tests for the Kling Lip Sync API, ensuring accurate billing at a flat rate of 2.45 Credits per call. Additionally, comprehensive integration guides have been added in both English and Chinese, along with updates to the service display pricing and API mapping. These enhancements improve usability and clarity for developers integrating with the Kling service.

items.title: Clean up async schema in Backend 🔧
items.summary: This update removes incorrect `async` schema blocks from the response examples in the `/qrart/tasks` and `/midjourney/tasks` endpoints. Although harmless, these blocks were unnecessary and have been verified to be the only changes made. All actual request-body parameters remain unaffected and correct.

items.title: Enhance Async Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enriches the service guides by adding the `async` parameter to 29 `development_*.md` files, clarifying its usage in relation to `callback_url`. Key changes include the addition of an `async` bullet in 24 standard guides and notes in 4 veo guides. This enhancement improves the clarity of asynchronous operations for users, ensuring they understand how to retrieve task IDs effectively.

items.title: Enhance Publish Console with New Connector Channels in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new channels—Medium, 百家号, X, and Facebook—into the `CHANNEL_CONNECTOR_IDENTIFIER`, allowing the publish console to render them as connector channels. The changes ensure that the status indicators and deep links are consistent with the AuthBackend and PlatformPublisher updates. No new internationalization keys were added.

items.title: Enhance Connector Credentials for Medium and Facebook in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new connectors for credential resolution in the Publisher. The Medium and Baijiahao channels now utilize a cookie connector path, while X and Facebook have been integrated with a new form-based credential fetching method. With 528 tests passing and code quality improvements, this enhancement streamlines the authentication process for these platforms.

items.title: Enhance publishing capabilities with new connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces additional publishing connectors for Medium, 百家号, X (formerly Twitter), and Facebook, expanding the connector catalog. Each connector utilizes a 'bring your own cookie' or API key method for authentication, allowing seamless integration with these platforms. The changes ensure that users can easily publish content across more channels, enhancing the overall functionality of the publish console.

items.title: New Lip Sync API for Kling Integration in Backend 🤖
items.summary: We've added a new public endpoint, **POST /kling/lip-sync**, which allows users to create synchronized audio or text-driven lip syncs for Kling-generated videos. This feature complements the existing `/kling/videos` endpoint, enabling a complete talking-photo pipeline. The API is set to cost 2.45 credits per call and includes comprehensive OpenAPI documentation.

items.title: Enhance Kling with Lip-Sync Endpoint in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces the new **POST /kling/lip-sync** endpoint to the ephone worker, enabling audio/text-driven lip-sync functionality via the official Kling API. It completes the talking-photo pipeline by allowing users to drive existing videos with audio or text, enhancing the overall multimedia experience. The implementation includes a new handler and updates to the server routing.

items.title: Enhance API Playground with Request Body Examples in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds top-level `requestBody.example` fields to 68 OpenAPI specs, ensuring that required fields are included in the API playground. Key endpoints like AI Chat and media services now have valid, runnable examples, improving user experience and reducing errors. The changes will take effect on the live site after a sync command is executed.

items.title: Streamline Async Submission for CLIs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the `adc`, `flux`, and `midjourney` CLIs to utilize the new `async: true` flag instead of a dummy health callback URL. The change eliminates unnecessary POST requests to the health endpoint, enhancing efficiency. Additionally, tests have been updated to reflect this new behavior, ensuring all assertions pass successfully.

items.title: Streamline Async Handling for Media MCPs in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the async handling for all 15 media MCP servers by replacing the dummy health callback with an explicit `async: true` flag. This change eliminates unnecessary POST requests to the health endpoint, improving efficiency. The codebase has been updated across multiple files, and all tests have been verified to pass successfully.

items.title: Enhance SDKs with Async Parameter in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `async` request parameter across Python, TypeScript, and Go SDKs, aligning with the new API-level `async: true` flag. The change allows methods to return a task ID immediately without needing a callback URL, enhancing the user experience for asynchronous operations. Modifications were made in multiple files, ensuring compatibility and successful builds across all languages.

items.title: Enhance Connector Dialog Deep-Linking in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces deep-linking functionality for the Connect, Reconnect, and Manage buttons in the publish console. Users will now be directed to the specific connector's dialog instead of the general connections homepage, improving navigation and user experience. The changes include updates to the connector service and the Channels component, ensuring a seamless integration with the AuthFrontend.

items.title: Enhance Connector Experience with Deep-Linking in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a deep-link feature that allows users to directly access a specific connector via the URL parameter `?connect=<connector_identifier>`. When this parameter is present, the corresponding connector's dialog will automatically open, streamlining the user experience by eliminating unnecessary navigation. The implementation includes type checking and linting for improved code quality.

items.title: Fix Publish Button Functionality in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The Publish button on the document console was non-functional due to a reference to an outdated computed property. This update replaces `recommendedConfigs` with the correct `recommendedTargets`, ensuring the button operates as intended. Type checking and linting have also been addressed for cleaner code.

items.title: Enhance Async Support for OpenAPI Specs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `async` parameter to all 43 OpenAPI specifications that support asynchronous task mode. Clients can now receive a `task_id` immediately without needing a `callback_url`, allowing them to poll for results via the service's tasks endpoint. The changes ensure that every relevant API now has a matching async parameter, enhancing the overall functionality and user experience.

items.title: Enhanced Async Support for Workers in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `async: true` support for 13 additional upstream workers, enhancing the request handling capabilities. The changes ensure that tasks can be processed asynchronously, allowing for immediate task IDs and improved client polling for results. Key modifications were made across multiple files to accommodate this feature while maintaining existing synchronous paths.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates from the zh-CN source. Key changes include modifications to multiple language files, ensuring consistent translations across Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese. This enhancement improves accessibility and user experience for a broader audience.

items.title: Enhance Publishing Experience with Connector and Live Task Board in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a channel-centric publish modal, allowing users to select connected channels without needing a config ID. The task board now features state filters and live auto-refresh, enhancing user interaction with ongoing tasks. Additionally, the update includes internationalization support for both English and Chinese, ensuring a broader accessibility.

items.title: Enhance API Playground with Required Fields in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the API playground by ensuring that required fields are synthesized even when they lack examples. The `schemaToExample()` function now generates valid request bodies, preventing errors during API calls. This change enhances the user experience by providing a reliable structure for requests, while optional fields remain unchanged for lean snippets.

items.title: Fix session status display for active sessions in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a bug where finished sessions were incorrectly flagged as 'Running' in the history drawer. The fix ensures that only sessions with a status of 'running' are marked as active, preventing idle sessions from being displayed as live. Comprehensive tests confirm that the display now accurately reflects session activity.

items.title: Enhance Seedream API with Async Parameter in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `async` boolean parameter to the Seedream Images API, allowing clients to receive a `task_id` immediately without needing a public `callback_url`. Users can now poll the `/seedream/tasks` endpoint to retrieve results, providing greater flexibility for those unable to host webhooks. The change is documented in both the OpenAPI definition and the Chinese documentation.

items.title: Enhance Async Mode Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an `async: true` flag for requests, allowing asynchronous task execution without needing a `callback_url`. Customers can now receive a `task_id` immediately and poll for results, improving flexibility for those without webhook endpoints. The change is part of a broader rollout across async-capable services, ensuring consistent behavior across the platform.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates from the zh-CN source. Key changes include modifications to multiple language files, enhancing the platform's multilingual support. The changes ensure that users across different languages receive consistent and accurate translations.

items.title: Enhanced Connector Status in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The publishing console now displays connection statuses for various platforms, enhancing user experience by linking connection actions to the connector system. Key updates include the integration of a connector operator for status reading and improved UI elements for managing connections. This update sets the stage for future enhancements, including a live feed and task board.

items.title: Enhance Free Access for Dreamina Tasks in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `/dreamina/tasks` endpoint to the `SKIP_AUTH_API_IDS`, allowing free access without authentication or billing. This change aligns the Dreamina service with other task endpoints, streamlining user experience. Key modifications were made in the settings file to implement this feature.

items.title: Introducing Dreamina: AI Talking-Photo Video Service in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update integrates the Dreamina digital-human video service, enabling audio-driven talking photos using ByteDance's OmniHuman 1.5 technology. Key features include a cost-effective API for generating lip-synced videos from a portrait image and audio, along with comprehensive documentation in both English and Chinese. The implementation includes new endpoints, pricing structures, and successful test cases to ensure functionality.

items.title: Exciting New Video Service for OmniHuman in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces the 即梦 (Jimeng) video service to the volcengine worker, enabling the generation of lip-synced videos from portrait images and audio. Key changes include the implementation of V4 HMAC-SHA256 signing and the addition of a new API endpoint for video processing. The feature has been end-to-end proven with successful output, enhancing the capabilities of the Platform Service.

items.title: Enhance Task Processing with Optional Config in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update allows connector tasks to proceed without a mandatory PublishConfig, enhancing flexibility in task processing. The `process_task` function now handles cases where `task.config` is None, allowing for faster failure with clear guidance if no credentials are resolved. A total of 523 tests have passed, ensuring stability and reliability in the changes made.

items.title: Enhance Connector Publishing by Making Config ID Optional in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the requirement for a `config_id` in connector-backed publish tasks, facilitating a smoother transition to connector-driven publishing. The `PublishTaskCreateSerializer` has been modified to allow `config_id` to be optional, enabling tasks to be created without a `PublishConfig`. Additionally, internal document links are now rewritten using the task's `user_id` when no config is provided.

items.title: Enhance Autofill Sync for Registration and Reset Forms in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a latent bug affecting the registration and reset-password forms where autofilled values were not triggering the necessary input events. The `syncAutofilledValues()` function has been applied to the email and phone fields in both forms, ensuring that the correct values are submitted on the first click. All relevant files have been updated and the codebase is clean with no linting errors.

items.title: Fix login autofill issue for smoother access in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a user-reported issue where the first click on the login button is ignored when credentials are autofilled. By adding references to the input fields, the values are now synchronized with the form before validation, ensuring that the login request is sent on the first click. This fix applies to both email and username login components.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Credential Handling for Publishers in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces OAuth support for the `linkedin` and `blogger` connectors, allowing them to fetch fresh bearer tokens from the AuthBackend. The changes include new functions for resolving OAuth tokens and adjustments to cookie handling, ensuring seamless integration with existing publisher workflows. A total of 20 connector tests were added, and all tests passed successfully.

items.title: Integrate PlatformStudio Submodule and Complete Phase-0 Spike in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update successfully integrates the PlatformStudio as a submodule, enhancing the article-to-video factory project. The Phase-0 spike has been moved to PlatformStudio, enabling it to drive the production renderer with real media, complete with burned-in captions. The plans directory now focuses on design and tracking, reflecting the latest developments in the project.

items.title: Burned-in Captions and Phase-0 Validation in Platform Studio 🎥
items.summary: This update integrates the Phase-0 spike into the rendering service, validating the renderer with real assets. Key changes include the addition of a burned-in caption renderer and enhancements to the FFmpeg build plan, allowing for seamless integration of captions in video outputs. The results demonstrate successful rendering of a 9:16 video with captions and voiceover, showcasing the capabilities of the updated pipeline.

items.title: Shrink empty-state magic-wand button for better UI in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update reduces the size of the empty-state magic-wand button from 48px to 40px, enhancing the overall user interface. The icon size has also been adjusted accordingly, while the button's functionality remains unchanged, still allowing users to open the config drawer on mobile. These changes aim to improve the visual appeal and usability of the component.

items.title: Improve WeChat Login Error Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where an invalid WeChat code exchange resulted in a 500 error instead of a proper 401 response. The code now checks for the presence of 'access_token' and 'openid' in the response, logging any errors and raising a LoginFailed exception when they are absent. This change enhances the stability of the WeChat login process by providing clearer error messaging to clients.

items.title: Enhanced API Descriptions with Translations in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces translatable field descriptions for all 144 API services, converting hardcoded OpenAPI descriptions into dynamic references. The zh-cn source text is now embedded in each file's translations block, ensuring consistency and accuracy across languages. Additionally, 171 description corrections have been made to enhance clarity and correctness in API documentation.

items.title: Streamline Login Error Logging in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the login error handling in the AuthBackend to prevent expected login failures from being logged as ERROR tracebacks. By re-raising API exceptions without logging them, we reduce noise in the logs and ensure that only genuine server errors are flagged. This change maintains the same client response behavior while improving log clarity and reducing alert fatigue.

items.title: Update Android Build Settings in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The default behavior for the `build-android` workflow has been updated to prevent automatic promotion to Production. Now, manual dispatches will default to the test track only, requiring explicit action to promote to Production. This change enhances control over release management for Android builds.

items.title: Revamp Service Detail Page with Global-First Hero and API Playground in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms the service detail page into a global-first hero, featuring a primary recharge button that ensures users have a universal application. Additionally, an inline API playground has been introduced, allowing users to interact with API snippets directly on the page. Key components have been reused to enhance maintainability and reduce duplication across the codebase.

items.title: Update iOS Build Process to Manual Review in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The default behavior for the `build-ios` dispatch has been changed to no longer auto-submit to Apple App Review. Now, it defaults to TestFlight-only unless explicitly set to auto-submit. This adjustment enhances control over the review process while keeping other distribution settings unchanged.

items.title: Optimize OpenAI Probes for Stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the E2E health probes for OpenAI chat and responses by implementing a curated model filter. The changes reduce false 500 errors and decrease probe traffic by five times, while maintaining customer-facing functionality. Key modifications include adjusting the probe model selection and increasing the test interval for better performance.

items.title: Streamline Credential Management by Removing Redundant Refresher in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the Publisher's cookie-refresh subsystem, streamlining credential management as the AuthBackend now handles the credential lifecycle. Key files related to the refresher have been deleted, including multiple Python scripts and configuration files. The change enhances system reliability by providing clearer error messages for expired cookies, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance Credential Management with Connector Vault in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update implements Phase 1 of the connector vault integration, prioritizing connector vault cookies over legacy configurations for improved credential management. Key changes include adjustments in `publish.py` to utilize the new `resolve_cookies` function and updates to the deployment configuration to include necessary environment variables. All tests have passed successfully, ensuring a smooth transition to this new system.

items.title: Unify Session Identity for Seamless Resuming in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates the session identity in the Coding Bridge, adopting the provider's real session ID for a unified experience. Key changes include improved session restoration, ensuring live sessions retain their state and functionality, such as the Stop button and typewriter effect. The redundant session fields have been collapsed into a single canonical ID, enhancing clarity and performance.

items.title: Streamlining Publisher with Connector Integration in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update outlines a staged development plan to transition PlatformPublisher's credential sourcing from its own configuration to the AuthBackend connector vault. Key phases include wiring in connector cookies, removing the existing cookie-refresh subsystem, and implementing end-to-end testing with real accounts. Documentation for each phase is provided to guide future implementation efforts.

items.title: Unify Session Identity for Seamless Resuming in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of dual identities in coding sessions by adopting a single canonical identity based on the provider's real ID. The changes ensure that live sessions can now reattach to their historical counterparts without spawning duplicate clients. Key modifications include re-keying sessions and marking live transcripts, enhancing user experience and session continuity.

items.title: Enhance History Drawer with Brand Icons in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the colored provider text tags in the coding-bridge history drawer with the actual brand logos for Claude and Codex. The change improves readability and aligns with the existing design patterns by using SVG assets. Key modifications include the introduction of a new `providerIcon()` helper and updated styles for a more compact display.

items.title: Enhance API Dialog with Service Favicon in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the **Try the API** dialog by displaying the selected service's favicon alongside its name in the dropdown. Utilizing Element Plus's `el-select` component, a new computed property retrieves the icon URL for the chosen service. This change enhances the user experience by providing a visual representation of the selected service.

items.title: Refine AI Chat Status Probe for Stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the AI Chat status probe to focus on a curated list of 11 stable production models, significantly reducing false outage signals. The probe's frequency has also been decreased from every 60 seconds to every 300 seconds, enhancing system performance. These changes aim to provide a more accurate representation of service availability and reduce unnecessary alerts.

items.title: Enhance API Translations with Dynamic Descriptions in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces translatable API field descriptions in the OpenAPI specification, allowing for dynamic localization. By integrating the existing translation pipeline, descriptions are now referenced via `$t()` keys, streamlining the process for multiple languages. The initial implementation focuses on the glm-chat-completions service, ensuring accurate translations are served based on user locale.

items.title: Optimize Checker for Efficiency in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update reduces the Checker CronJob's probe interval from every 2 minutes to every 10 minutes, significantly lowering the number of probes. Additionally, the prompt for checking liveness has been simplified to a one-word response, drastically cutting token usage and costs associated with upstream services. These changes enhance performance while maintaining acceptable detection lag.

items.title: Improve BYOC Credential Error Handling in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the error handling for BYOC credentials by implementing a new function, `formatErrorDetail()`, which flattens nested error objects into a readable string. Previously, errors were not displayed, leading to confusion for users. Now, both extension and form submission errors will be clearly communicated, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance cookie handling in Backend 🍪
items.summary: This update modifies the `ConnectorCredentialsSerializer` to accept list values for cookies in the BYOC credential payload. By changing the `payload` field to a `DictField`, both list-shaped and string-shaped payloads are now supported, ensuring proper validation and functionality for cookie-based connectors. This fix resolves the 400 error encountered during connection attempts.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications were made across multiple language files, enhancing the application's accessibility for users in various regions. The changes include new confirmation messages and error handling prompts in several languages.

items.title: Enhance Deployment Deletion with Force Option in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a force delete option for deployment-type applications when normal deletion fails. Users will now see a confirmation prompt asking if they want to force delete the application record, ensuring that stuck records can be removed. Key changes include modifications to the application operator and UI components to handle this new functionality.

items.title: Enhance deployment teardown with force option in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a `?force=true` parameter to the deployment teardown API, allowing the deletion of application records even when the underlying cloud resource fails to tear down. This feature provides operators with a solution for handling orphaned applications with corrupted metadata or unreachable resources. The change ensures that applications can be removed without leaving undeletable records in the system.

items.title: Revamped Landing Page with Enhanced Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the landing page, including a new installation step guide for SDKs, enhanced code blocks using a unified `CodeSnippet` component, and visually appealing API category cards with icons. Additionally, the tab night mode has been fixed for better visibility, and all code snippets have been verified for accuracy against existing packages. The overall user experience is now more engaging and informative.

items.title: Boosted AI Models Count on Homepage in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The homepage of UnifiedHub has been updated to reflect an increase in AI models from 200+ to 300+. This change involved modifications to the UnifiedHub component and localization files for both English and Chinese. The update ensures that users have the most accurate information regarding the current catalog size.

items.title: Add gear icon for Manage Keys button in Frontend Web ⚙️
items.summary: This update registers the `faGear` icon for the **管理密钥** (Manage Keys) button on the `/console/applications` page. Previously, this button rendered without an icon, while other buttons displayed theirs. The fix involved importing and adding the `faGear` icon to the central Font Awesome plugin, ensuring consistent iconography across the interface.

items.title: Enlarge Application ID Font Size for Better Readability in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update increases the font size of the application ID displayed on the global balance card in the console from 11px to 13px. The change ensures consistency with surrounding text, enhancing overall readability for users. The modification was made in the ApiKeyQuickAccess component.

items.title: Removed cookie keep-alive feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The cookie keep-alive feature has been removed due to build issues with the Playwright image. This change eliminates several components including the Dockerfile, cronjob, and associated services, while ensuring that cookie connectors remain functional. The update streamlines the deployment process and maintains existing cookie READ functionality.

items.title: Enhance session settings restoration and editability in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update restores session settings such as model, working directory, reasoning effort, and edit mode when reloading a Coding Bridge conversation. Users can now edit these settings on every turn, ensuring a more flexible and consistent experience. The changes are complemented by server-side updates to persist these settings effectively.

items.title: Enhance Session Management in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new session metadata management system that persists and restores per-session settings, including working directory, permission mode, and reasoning effort. Key files modified include `session_meta.py`, which now saves session data to a JSON file, and updates to `session.py` and `connection.py` to integrate this functionality. Overall, this enhancement improves the user experience by ensuring that sessions can resume with the correct context after a reload.

items.title: Enhance cookie image for seamless playwright installs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Dockerfile for the cookie keep-alive image to ensure that the apt lists are retained during the playwright installation process. By moving the cleanup step after the installation, it prevents errors related to missing package lists, thereby enhancing the build process without impacting app deployment. This change is part of ongoing improvements to the deployment workflow.

items.title: Update connector icons for global accessibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update re-hosts all connector icons on the CDN to ensure they load properly across different regions. Previously, some icons were blocked or failed to render due to cross-origin restrictions. Now, all 11 icons are accessible regardless of the user's location, enhancing the user experience.

items.title: Enhance AskUserQuestion Feedback in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of user responses in the AskUserQuestion feature. It ensures that structured answers are correctly passed through the system, allowing the CLI to acknowledge user input instead of indicating that no response was given. New tests have been added to verify this functionality, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Enhance Cookie-Capture Extension with Auth Match in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `auth.acedata.cloud` content-script match for the cookie-capture extension, allowing it to inject on the connectors page. The version is bumped to v0.3, and TypeScript build issues have been resolved, ensuring a clean repackage. The installation documentation has also been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Update acedataext Install Docs for v0.3 Package in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The installation documentation for the acedataext browser extension has been updated to point to the new v0.3 package. This change affects all 18 locales, ensuring users download the correct file that includes the necessary content-script match for auth.acedata.cloud. The live documentation will reflect this update after the merge.

items.title: Fix AskUserQuestion Answer Handling in Coding Bridge Agent 🤖
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where answers to AskUserQuestion prompts were not being processed correctly, leading to misleading messages. Key changes include plumbing the answer through the system and updating the PermissionBroker to handle answers appropriately. New tests have been added to ensure functionality and verify that the CLI now correctly echoes user responses.
